name: CNH Industrial N.V.

profile.description: CNH Industrial N.V. designs, manufactures, and distributes agricultural and construction equipment under Case IH, New Holland, STEYR, and Case Construction brands globally. The company operates through Agriculture, Construction, and Financial Services segments, with precision agriculture solutions and AI-powered field technologies. Founded in 1842, headquartered in Basildon, UK. Q1 2026 revenue of $3.8B was essentially flat YoY, but net income fell to $10M from $132M due to tariff impacts and volume declines. Market cap $13.0B.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): CNH completed its spin-off from Iveco in 2022, creating a pure-play ag/construction company, and has introduced 15+ new tractor models and 30+ precision tech releases planned through 2027 [1]. However, these are incremental product refreshes rather than transformative business model changes. AI Deployment (2/2): Every CNH global leadership team member is running at least one generative or agentic AI pilot program [2]. The AI Tech Assistant was deployed to hundreds of agricultural and construction dealer groups in early 2025 for real-time diagnostic support. AI-enabled spraying systems use machine learning to reduce herbicide application by 80%, delivering measurable customer value [2]. Market Share (1/2): CNH holds the #2/#3 global position in agricultural equipment (behind Deere, alongside AGCO) with strong brand equity through Case IH and New Holland. However, market share has been pressured during the 2024-2026 agricultural downturn with declining volumes across regions [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): CNH targets 90% of precision technologies manufactured in-house by 2030 (up from 80%), with specific refreshed 2026 agricultural lineup and pipeline visibility through 2027 [2]. Timelines are specific but revenue visibility is limited by cyclical demand uncertainty. R&D Intensity (1/1): R&D expenses increased in Q1 2026 despite flat revenue, demonstrating counter-cyclical innovation investment [1].

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): CNH's 'Breaking New Ground' purpose connects equipment manufacturing to sustainable food production and infrastructure development. CEO Gerrit Marx has framed this explicitly — 'tomorrow's agronomist will be agentic AI helping farmers make bigger decisions better' [2]. Clear and thoughtful, connecting the company's work to global food security, but not yet validated by decades of consistent execution under this framing. Community Impact (1/2): CNH achieved the highest score (88/100) among 126 companies in S&P Global's Machinery and Electrical Equipment CSA, with 11 consecutive years in DJSI World and Europe indices [3]. Demonstrates systematic ESG commitment rather than distinctive community philanthropy. Employee Retention (0/2): No specific retention or tenure data publicly available. CEO transition from Marx's appointment in July 2024 and subsequent leadership reorganization creates uncertainty. Insider Buying (0/2): No evidence of meaningful insider buying by executives identified in recent filings.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Liquidity check: 15,614,263 avg daily volume x $10.46 = $163.3M daily dollar volume — passes easily [1]. Mechanically scored as Growth stock (current yield 0.96% < 2%): D/E 333.7% vs target <=70%, floor 300% → score = (300-333.7)/(300-70) = -0.15 → capped at 0.0 (1x). ROE 4.84% vs target >=15%, floor 0% → score = 4.84/15 = 0.32 (2x). Revenue Growth YoY -0.1% vs target >=10%, floor -20% → score = 19.9/30 = 0.66 (1x). Revenue Growth 5yr CAGR: excluded (insufficient data). Forward P/E 14.3 vs target <=20, floor 60 → score = 45.7/40 = 1.14 → 1.0 (1x). Profit Margin 2.13% vs target >=20%, floor 0% → score = 2.13/20 = 0.11 (1x). Forward PEG 0.59 vs target <=1.0, floor 3.0 → score = 2.41/2.0 = 1.21 → 1.0 (2x). Weighted sum: 0.0 + 0.646 + 0.663 + 1.0 + 0.107 + 2.0 = 4.42. Max weighted: 8. Infrastructure = round(4.42/8 x 10) = round(5.52) = 6.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): CNH manufactures physical agricultural and construction equipment — tractors, combines, excavators, dozers — that requires capital-intensive manufacturing, global supply chains, dealer networks, and financing operations that AI cannot substitute [1]. The business involves complex physical products with significant aftermarket services creating recurring revenue. Demand for farm equipment is driven by global food production imperatives that AI amplifies rather than replaces — AI in agriculture increases demand for smarter equipment, not less equipment. The only marginal AI risk is potential disruption to CNH's Financial Services segment by AI-powered lending, but this is outweighed by the physical product moat. AI Opportunity (1/1): CNH generates measurable value from AI deployments — the AI Tech Assistant serves hundreds of dealer groups, AI-enabled spraying reduces herbicide use by 80%, and every global leadership team member is running at least one generative or agentic AI pilot [2]. CEO Marx has committed to 90% in-house precision technology by 2030.

infinite_game.summary: CNH has genuine long-term purpose around sustainable agriculture and food security, reinforced by CEO Marx's explicit framing of AI as a 'generational shift' in farming productivity [2]. The company's 182-year heritage since 1842 demonstrates institutional endurance. However, the current downturn response has been cautious — managing costs and protecting near-term margins rather than investing aggressively through the cycle. The 2026 guidance of flat-to-down-4% with adjusted EPS of $0.35-$0.45 reflects finite-game earnings management during a cyclical trough [1].

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): CNH's 'Breaking New Ground' purpose connects equipment manufacturing to the challenge of feeding a growing world sustainably, and CEO Marx has explicitly framed precision agriculture and AI as generational missions [2]. However, this cause is shared by every agricultural equipment company — Deere's 'leap-frogging' strategy and AGCO's precision ag investments articulate similar visions. The purpose is genuine but not distinctive enough to constitute a true Just Cause that drives strategic decisions others wouldn't make.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): CNH completed strategic leadership reorganization under CEO Marx (appointed July 2024) with a simplified matrix structure and empowered Global Leadership Team [2]. The company employs 34,197 people globally with generally stable operations. However, the recent CEO transition adds uncertainty, and the 2026 earnings collapse (Q1 net income down 92% to $10M) creates internal organizational stress [1].

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): CNH explicitly benchmarks against John Deere's precision agriculture leadership and uses competitive gaps to drive its own technology roadmap [2]. Management language focuses on delivering better products and closing capability gaps rather than defeating competitors. CEO Marx's commitment to 90% in-house precision tech by 2030 is framed as a self-improvement objective, not a competitive attack.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): The 2022 spin-off of Iveco Group to create a pure-play ag/construction company was a meaningful strategic simplification [1]. The ongoing transformation from pure 'iron' manufacturer to technology-integrated precision agriculture platform represents incremental but genuine evolution. However, these shifts were industry-following rather than industry-defining.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): CNH maintained 15+ new tractor launches and increased R&D spending in Q1 2026 despite the worst agricultural equipment downturn in decades [1][2]. CEO Marx took direct personal responsibility for the Agriculture segment as CEO, signaling prioritization. However, the company's 2026 guidance of $0.35-$0.45 EPS reflects cautious cost management rather than bold counter-cyclical investment.

investment_thesis: CNH Industrial is a high-quality global franchise navigating a cyclical trough with clear recovery potential. At 14.3x forward P/E and 0.59 forward PEG, the stock prices in significant earnings decline with Q1 2026 net income of $10M (down 92% YoY) representing near-trough profitability [1]. The precision agriculture technology stack — AI Tech Assistant deployed to hundreds of dealers, AI spraying reducing herbicide use 80%, every global leader running AI pilots — positions CNH to capture higher-margin technology value as the cycle turns [2]. Agricultural demand recovery expected in 2027 as dealer inventory destocking completes and farmer income normalizes. The 182-year heritage, strong brand portfolio (Case IH, New Holland), and Inspire Impact Score of 64 (ALIGNED) make this a compelling faith-aligned cyclical recovery candidate.
thesis_continued: Near-term headwinds are severe. Q1 2026 Agriculture adjusted EBIT collapsed to $27M from $139M, driven by tariff impacts (210-220bps margin drag on agriculture, 600bps on construction), lower North America and South America volumes, and higher R&D spending [1]. Full-year 2026 guidance of flat-to-down-4% revenue and $0.35-$0.45 adjusted EPS represents a 57-67% decline from 2025's already depressed $1.05, making this potentially the worst earnings year in the current cycle. D/E of 333.7% (driven by Financial Services leverage) exceeds IOWN's 300% floor, and profit margin of 2.13% is far below the 20% target. The dividend has been cut from $0.47/share in 2024 to $0.10 annualized in 2026, effectively eliminating income appeal [1].
key_catalysts: Agricultural equipment demand recovery beginning in 2027 as dealer inventory destocking completes and normalized commodity prices support farmer capital expenditure decisions

Precision agriculture technology monetization — AI Tech Assistant and integrated crop management platform driving software/service revenue above hardware-only margins as adoption scales across dealer networks
Margin recovery operating leverage: high-horsepower equipment volumes showing the deepest declines are also the most profitable segment, creating significant earnings leverage on any volume recovery
key_risks: Agricultural downturn extending beyond 2027 if elevated interest rates, trade policy uncertainty, and weak commodity prices delay farmer equipment purchases, creating further earnings pressure below the $0.35-$0.45 guided range

Tariff escalation: CNH expects 210-220bps agricultural margin drag and 600bps construction margin drag from current tariff rules, with risk of further deterioration if trade tensions worsen across US, EU, Brazil, and India manufacturing footprint

Competitive disadvantage vs. John Deere in autonomous/electric equipment and precision agriculture software monetization — Deere's technology leadership could widen during CNH's earnings trough when R&D budgets are constrained
